presentation.chiefComplaint: Right lower abdominal pain for one day with nausea

history.hpi: Pain began around the umbilicus yesterday and migrated to the right lower quadrant, now sharp and worse with movement. Nausea, anorexia, and one episode of vomiting. Low-grade fever. Last menstrual period three weeks ago.
redFlags: Migratory RLQ pain with peritoneal signs (concern for appendicitis)
Multiple resources expected (labs, imaging, IV analgesia)

expert.esiRationale: Stable vital signs with no immediate or high-risk threat, but the presentation requires multiple resources (labs, urine/serum hCG, CT or ultrasound, IV fluids, analgesia, surgical consult). Two-or-more resources with stable vitals -> ESI 3.

provenance.sourceRef: ED Triage Trainer hand-authored seed case
